vocab.meaning: chi tiền mạnh tay vào thứ gì đó (thường là xa xỉ)
vocab.example: I'm usually frugal, but I decided to splash out on a pair of designer leather boots last month.

vocab.meaning: một số tiền cực kỳ lớn
vocab.example: While some limited-edition sneakers cost a king's ransom, I prefer keeping my spending within a reasonable limit.

vocab.meaning: thắt lưng buộc bụng, tiết kiệm chi tiêu
vocab.example: I’ve had to tighten my belt recently, so I haven't bought any new footwear for over six months.

vocab.meaning: tốn quá nhiều tiền, làm khánh kiệt tài chính
vocab.example: I look for high-quality shoes that are durable but won't necessarily break the bank.
I try to keep track of my monthly expenses, so I rarely overspend on impulsive shoe purchases.

vocab.meaning: lên tới con số (thường là một số tiền lớn)
vocab.example: Every year, I probably invest in footwear to the tune of five hundred dollars.

vocab.meaning: trả một giá quá đắt, bị chém giá
vocab.example: If you buy shoes at the airport, you’ll likely pay through the nose compared to high street prices.

vocab.meaning: rất giàu có, tiền tiêu không hết
vocab.example: I’m not exactly rolling in it, so I wait for the end-of-season sales to grab a bargain.
These running shoes were expensive, but given their comfort and support, they were worth every penny.

vocab.meaning: tiết kiệm cho những lúc khó khăn/cần kíp
vocab.example: Instead of buying a new pair every month, I prefer to save for a rainy day and only shop when necessary.
